title: corylopsis velutina
intro: Corylopsis velutina, also known as the hairy winter hazel, is a deciduous shrub native to Japan and China, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and velvety leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Corylopsis velutina include reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, making it a valuable remedy for the common cold and flu. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to soothe sore throats and calm irritated mucous membranes.
